notes: Installed and started right up.  Dropped the graphics down to medium for decent framerate.  Played through the tutorial and first mission without any problems.
title: Red Faction Guerrilla Steam Edition

notes: Works out of the box on Ubuntu MATE 18.04.2 LTS and Logitech F710 works properly.
title: Red Faction Guerrilla Steam Edition

notes: The performance is choppy when blowing lots of things up but otherwise runs perfectly
title: Red Faction Guerrilla Steam Edition

notes: Installs and runs great! No problems I can think of. What a great game, too.
title: Red Faction Guerrilla Steam Edition

notes:
Played through the whole game very comfortably. Performance is great. The only issue is a weird graphical glitch - whenever you look straight up at the sky, you see a black area. This isn't really a big issue, since you don't need to look at this angle very often, but it was sometimes annoying when fighting flyers.

I tried various tweaks (including running the game in DX9 mode), but none of them helped. The Re-Mars-tered Edition has this issue as well.
title: Red Faction Guerrilla Steam Edition

notes:
Platinum only in the most literal sense - it *runs* with zero issues - unlike the remastered version.
Playability, however, is not great; it hitches and bogs even with heavily lowered settings, and destroying even one building slashes framerate, which in a destruction-centered game effectively makes the title almost unplayable. The performance delta between Proton and Windows for this title is abysmal.
title: Red Faction Guerrilla Steam Edition
